summary: The test suite is effectively non-functional: 51 of 52 tests fail with API timeouts or HTML error responses (HTTP 503 / DOCTYPE responses), indicating the search API itself is unreachable or returning error pages instead of JSON. No meaningful search-quality signal can be extracted until the service availability issue is resolved.

strengths.title: One test passing proves end-to-end path works
strengths.detail: 1/52 tests passed, confirming the test harness, fixture format, and at least one code path through hybridSearch can return valid results when the API responds.

strengths.title: Fast p50 latency on responsive requests
strengths.detail: p50 of 77ms suggests that when the API does respond, the underlying Meilisearch + vector retrieval is performant; the p95 of 1092ms reflects timeout/error cases rather than slow queries.

critical_gaps.title: API returning HTML error pages instead of JSON
critical_gaps.detail: Nearly every failure is 'Unexpected token <, "<!DOCTYPE "...' meaning the test client is hitting an HTML error page (likely a reverse proxy 502/503/504 or a Next.js error page) rather than the JSON search endpoint. This is an infrastructure/routing failure, not a relevance failure.
critical_gaps.evidence: 50/52 failures show DOCTYPE parse errors; tablet-of-wisdom-nature explicitly shows HTTP 503. All categories affected uniformly (phrase-match, concept-match, authority-ranking, cross-tradition, entity-aware).

critical_gaps.title: Failure classification masks real signal
critical_gaps.detail: All 51 failures are categorized as 'API timeout' in the breakdown, but the actual errors are 503s and HTML responses — these are distinct failure modes. The test runner needs to distinguish between true timeouts, HTTP error codes, and malformed responses.
critical_gaps.evidence: Failure breakdown shows 51 'API timeout' but error messages indicate HTTP 503 and HTML responses, not actual timeouts.

critical_gaps.title: No relevance signal recoverable
critical_gaps.detail: Because the API is failing before search runs, we cannot evaluate BM25/vector hybrid quality, authority weighting, or the jafar-pipeline intent→research→craft flow. All downstream quality work is blocked.
critical_gaps.evidence: 0% pass on concept-match, authority-ranking, cross-tradition, entity-aware categories — all bottlenecked at the HTTP layer.

action_plan.title: Diagnose and fix API 503 / HTML response root cause
action_plan.description: The API endpoint is returning HTML (likely from a proxy or Next.js error page) instead of JSON. Until this is fixed, no search-quality work can proceed. Most likely causes: Meilisearch connection failure, missing env vars, or the API process crashing under test load.
action_plan.implementation: 1) curl the search endpoint manually with one of the failing queries (e.g., the tablet-of-wisdom-nature query) and inspect the full HTML response — it will reveal whether this is nginx/Caddy/Vercel proxy or Next.js error. 2) Check api/lib/search.js hybridSearch for unhandled exceptions; wrap Meilisearch client calls in try/catch and return JSON errors. 3) Verify MEILI_HOST, MEILI_API_KEY, and OpenAI API key env vars are set in the deployed environment. 4) Check Meilisearch health endpoint and confirm indexes exist and are not still syncing (note from changelog: 'sync queue clearing').
action_plan.success_metric: Reduce DOCTYPE/503 errors from 51 to 0; expose true search-quality pass rate (expected jump to baseline ~40-60% based on prior 58% core figure in changelog).

action_plan.title: Improve test runner error classification
action_plan.description: Current failure breakdown lumps HTTP 503, JSON parse failures, and real timeouts under 'API timeout'. This makes triage impossible. Split into distinct buckets.
action_plan.implementation: In the test runner (likely tests/run.js or similar), catch fetch errors and classify: (a) network/timeout, (b) HTTP non-2xx with status code, (c) JSON.parse failure with response preview, (d) result-level mismatch. Log first 200 chars of body on parse failure to identify proxy vs app errors fast.
action_plan.success_metric: Future failure reports show actionable categories; eliminates ambiguous 'API timeout' bucket.

action_plan.title: Add /health and /search smoke tests to CI gate
action_plan.description: A simple precondition check before running the 52-fixture suite would have flagged this in seconds rather than producing a misleading 2% quality score.
action_plan.implementation: Prepend the test runner with a single GET to /api/search?q=test that asserts 200 + valid JSON shape. If it fails, abort the suite with a clear infrastructure-error message rather than running 52 fixtures.
action_plan.success_metric: Future infrastructure outages produce 'API unreachable' aborts instead of polluting quality metrics.

action_plan.title: Confirm Meilisearch index population post-sync
action_plan.description: The changelog notes the Meilisearch sync queue was clearing. If the 503 is caused by an empty or partially-populated index, fixing the API alone won't restore the prior 58% core pass rate.
action_plan.implementation: After API is reachable, query Meilisearch stats endpoint (/indexes/{name}/stats) to confirm documentCount matches expected corpus size. Verify both BM25 index and pgvector embeddings table have parity rows. Re-run any failed sync tasks.
action_plan.success_metric: Restore pass rate to or above the 58% baseline noted in 2026-05-31 changelog entry.

notes_on_previous: No prior analyses exist. The changelog mentions a 58% core baseline on 2026-05-31, but the current 2% result and uniform 503/HTML failures strongly suggest a regression in deployment or infrastructure rather than a search-quality regression. Do not begin tuning BM25 weights, authority multipliers, or rerank logic until the API is confirmed to be returning JSON — any quality changes made now would be evaluated against noise.

summary: Pass rate has recovered from 2% to 56% now that the API is returning JSON, with authority-ranking (100%) and cross-tradition (100%) performing perfectly. The biggest gaps are entity-aware queries (22%) suffering from frequent timeouts and concept-match (42%) where queries return rank-1 hits but fail text-match assertions — suggesting the right *kind* of document is found but not the specific target passage.

strengths.title: Authority weighting works as designed
strengths.detail: authority-ranking category is 100% (4/4) and authority_hit=true on essentially every failing test, confirming the post-retrieval authority multiplier in api/lib/authority.js is correctly preferring high-authority sources.

strengths.title: Cross-tradition routing is solid
strengths.detail: cross-tradition is 100% (5/5), indicating the hybrid BM25+semantic stack handles multi-corpus disambiguation well when queries are sufficiently distinctive.

strengths.title: Massive recovery from infra regression
strengths.detail: Pass rate jumped 2% → 56% after the API/HTML-response issue was resolved, validating that the prior analysis correctly diagnosed an infra problem rather than a quality regression.

critical_gaps.title: Entity-aware queries timing out
critical_gaps.detail: 5 of 9 entity-aware tests fail with 'operation aborted due to timeout' at p95=25s. These are likely triggering the full jafar-pipeline (intent → research → craft) which compounds latency, and entity resolution may be issuing too many sub-queries.
critical_gaps.evidence: entity-quddus-character-tracking, entity-letters-of-living, entity-shoghi-effendi-guardian, entity-bab-martyrdom, entity-mulla-husayn-speaker all error with timeout

critical_gaps.title: Concept-match returns rank-1 but wrong passage
critical_gaps.detail: 11 concept-match failures have rank=1 with text_hit=false. The retriever finds the correct book/author (e.g., Mahabharata for dharma, Matthew for golden rule, Sura II for zakat) but surfaces a non-target paragraph. This is a within-document ranking / paragraph-selection problem, not a retrieval problem.
critical_gaps.evidence: christian-golden-rule (Matthew, rank 1, text miss), islamic-zakat-charity (Sura II, rank 1, text miss), hindu-dharma (Mahabharata 12, rank 1, text miss), buddhist-compassion-karuna, islamic-daily-prayers

critical_gaps.title: Phrase-match misses on archaic/liturgical phrasing
critical_gaps.detail: Phrase-match queries with stylized scripture language (e.g. 'cleanse thy heart', 'Inaccessible Most High Most Holy') return null rank entirely — the target paragraph is not even in top 10. Embedding similarity likely dominates over rare-phrase BM25 here.
critical_gaps.evidence: hidden-words-cleanse-heart, long-healing-prayer-opening, aqdas-obligatory-prayer, seven-valleys-law-secret-path all rank=null

critical_gaps.title: p95 latency at 25s is unworkable
critical_gaps.detail: p50 6.4s and p95 25s indicate test timeouts (likely 25s client limit) are the direct cause of 6 errored tests. Even passing tests are slow enough to mask other regressions.

action_plan.title: Bypass jafar-pipeline for test/eval path and add per-stage timeouts
action_plan.description: The 6 timeout failures are concentrated in entity-aware tests, which likely fan out through the intent→research→craft pipeline. Add a 'raw' or 'eval' mode that runs hybridSearch directly, and wrap each pipeline stage with AbortController timeouts (e.g., 4s intent, 8s research) so a slow stage degrades gracefully rather than hanging.
action_plan.implementation: In api/lib/jafar-pipeline.js, accept an options.mode='raw' to skip intent+craft and return hybridSearch results directly. In the test runner, pass mode=raw for ranking tests. Add Promise.race with AbortController in each stage. Log per-stage latency.
action_plan.success_metric: Eliminate all 6 timeout errors; entity-aware category climbs from 22% toward 60%+; p95 drops below 10s.

action_plan.title: Boost BM25 weight and enable phrase/proximity matching for archaic queries
action_plan.description: Four phrase-match failures (cleanse thy heart, Inaccessible Most High, secret of the path, etc.) return rank=null, indicating semantic embeddings are pulling toward thematically similar but lexically wrong paragraphs. Rare-token phrases should be retrieved by BM25, not embeddings.
action_plan.implementation: In api/lib/search.js hybridSearch, increase BM25 weight in the fusion (try 0.6 BM25 / 0.4 vector vs current). Enable Meilisearch phrase matching ('"..."') when query contains ≥3 rare tokens (IDF-based heuristic). Consider Meilisearch attribute weights so paragraph text outranks title.
action_plan.success_metric: Phrase-match category 67% → 85%+; recover hidden-words-cleanse-heart, long-healing-prayer-opening, aqdas-obligatory-prayer.

action_plan.title: Improve paragraph-level reranking within correctly-retrieved documents
action_plan.description: 11 concept-match failures find rank-1 results from the *correct* book/author but the wrong paragraph. Add a second-stage reranker that scores paragraph-query overlap using token-level features (exact token hits, n-gram overlap, position of key terms) before authority weighting.
action_plan.implementation: In api/lib/authority.js (or new api/lib/rerank.js), after authority multiplication, apply a paragraph-quality boost: count of query tokens present in paragraph / paragraph length, with bonus for bigram matches. Tune on the 11 failing concept-match cases.
action_plan.success_metric: Concept-match category 42% → 65%+; specifically recover christian-golden-rule, hindu-dharma, islamic-zakat-charity.

action_plan.title: Add entity index and entity-aware query expansion
action_plan.description: Entity queries like 'Declaration of the Bab May 1844 Shiraz' or 'first Letter of the Living' need entity-grounded retrieval, not just lexical/semantic. A small entity dictionary (Bab, Mulla Husayn, Quddus, Shoghi Effendi, Letters of the Living, etc.) with associated canonical passages/aliases would dramatically help.
action_plan.implementation: Create data/entities.json with entity → {aliases, canonical_passages, related_terms}. In api/lib/search.js, detect entity mentions in query and OR-expand with aliases; boost paragraphs whose entity tags match. Eventually backfill an entity field on paragraphs.
action_plan.success_metric: Entity-aware category 22% → 60%+; recover entity-mulla-husayn-first-believer (currently rank 7), entity-declaration-bab-1844.

action_plan.title: Split timeout failure class and add stage-level metrics
action_plan.description: Continue the previous run's recommendation: distinguish client-side AbortController timeouts from server 5xx and pipeline-stage timeouts. Without this, P1 above is hard to verify.
action_plan.implementation: In the test runner, capture which stage timed out (header from API: X-Pipeline-Stage). Bucket errors as timeout-intent, timeout-retrieval, timeout-craft, http-5xx.
action_plan.success_metric: Clearer failure breakdown; faster iteration on P1.

notes_on_previous: The previous analysis correctly identified the 2% pass rate as an infra problem and explicitly warned against tuning quality knobs against noise — that restraint paid off, and the recovery to 56% confirms the underlying search stack was healthy. The recommendation to add /health smoke tests and improve error classification remains unimplemented; carrying that forward (P5) is still cheap and worthwhile. No dead ends to avoid — prior analysis made no quality-tuning changes.

summary: Pass rate has climbed to 71% (37/52) with MRR 0.798 and zero timeouts/5xx — the infra is healthy and authority-ranking (100%) and cross-tradition (100%) are solved. The dominant failure mode is now text-mismatch (10/15 failures): the right *kind* of document is retrieved at rank 1 but the wrong paragraph, plus 5 entity-aware queries that miss the top 10 entirely.

strengths.title: Infrastructure fully stabilized
strengths.detail: Zero timeouts, zero HTTP errors, zero network errors across all 52 fixtures. p50 of 4.5s and p95 of 9.7s are workable (p95 down from ~25s in the prior run).

strengths.title: Authority and cross-tradition routing solid
strengths.detail: authority-ranking 100% (4/4) and cross-tradition 100% (5/5) — the authority weighting and multi-tradition retrieval logic are correctly tuned and need no further work.

strengths.title: Correct document retrieval even on failures
strengths.detail: Almost every concept/phrase failure has top_hit_author or top_hit_title from the correct tradition or author (e.g. Bahá'u'lláh for tablet-of-ahmad, Muhammad for islamic-hadith-patience). Retrieval is finding the right corpus region; only paragraph-level selection is wrong.

critical_gaps.title: Paragraph-level reranking is the bottleneck
critical_gaps.detail: 10 of 15 failures are 'text mismatch' at rank 1 — meaning hybrid search picks the correct book but the wrong passage within it. Authority weighting may even be hurting here by pulling toward high-authority paragraphs that don't contain the target tokens.
critical_gaps.evidence: tablet-of-ahmad-nightingale (rank 1, wrong tablet by same author), islamic-daily-prayers (rank 1, wrong sura), christian-beatitudes-meek (rank 1, Apocrypha instead of Matthew 5), hindu-bhagavad-gita-action (rank 1, Mahabharata 6 instead of Gita)

critical_gaps.title: Entity-aware queries still miss top 10
critical_gaps.detail: 5 entity queries return rank=null. Top hits drift to generic Bahá'u'lláh works or Shoghi Effendi histories rather than passages naming the specific entity (Mullá Husayn, the Báb's declaration, Letters of the Living).
critical_gaps.evidence: entity-mulla-husayn-first-believer, entity-declaration-bab-1844, entity-letters-of-living, entity-bab-martyrdom, shoghi-effendi-progressive-revelation (all rank=null)

critical_gaps.title: Canonical-text queries routed to wrong canonical book
critical_gaps.detail: Famous phrases match the *theme* via semantic similarity but land in adjacent canonical books. Golden Rule and Beatitudes go to Apocrypha; Bhagavad Gita verses go to other Mahabharata books; Islamic ritual queries go to Quranic tafsir rather than the ritual-prescriptive verse.
critical_gaps.evidence: christian-golden-rule → Apocrypha; christian-beatitudes-meek → Apocrypha; hindu-bhagavad-gita-action → Mahabharata 6; islamic-zakat-charity → Sura II rather than zakat-prescriptive verse

action_plan.title: Add token-overlap paragraph reranker before authority weighting
action_plan.description: 10 of 15 failures are wrong-paragraph-in-right-book. Add a second-stage reranker that scores each candidate paragraph on query-token coverage (exact lemma hits, rare-term IDF, bigram overlap) and blend it with the current authority×match score. This directly attacks the dominant failure class without touching retrieval.
action_plan.implementation: In api/lib/authority.js (or new api/lib/paragraph-rerank.js), after hybridSearch returns the top ~50 candidates, compute per-paragraph: (a) fraction of query content tokens present, (b) sum of IDF for matched rare tokens, (c) tightest-window proximity score. Combine as final_score = 0.45*lexical_overlap + 0.30*semantic + 0.15*authority + 0.10*match_quality. Gate behind RERANK_V2=1 env var so it can be A/B'd.

action_plan.title: Build entity dictionary with canonical-passage anchors
action_plan.description: All 5 rank=null failures are entity queries. A static entity table mapping named entities (Mullá Husayn, Letters of the Living, Báb, Declaration 1844, Tabriz martyrdom) to canonical passage IDs in God Passes By, Dawn-Breakers, etc., gives a deterministic retrieval boost when the query mentions the entity.
action_plan.implementation: Create api/data/entities.json with entries like {name, aliases[], canonical_paragraph_ids[], boost}. In api/lib/search.js hybridSearch, after query normalization, detect entity mentions via case-insensitive alias match and either (a) inject canonical_paragraph_ids into the candidate pool with a +1.5 score bump, or (b) add an OR clause to the Meilisearch query targeting those IDs. Start with ~30 high-value Bahá'í entities covered by the failing tests.

action_plan.title: Boost BM25/phrase weight for queries with rare canonical tokens
action_plan.description: Queries like 'nightingale of paradise singeth upon the twigs' and 'Blessed are the meek' contain rare lexical signatures that BM25 should pin immediately but semantic embeddings dilute. Detect high-IDF query tokens and shift the hybrid weight toward keyword for those queries.
action_plan.implementation: In api/lib/search.js hybridSearch, compute mean IDF of query content tokens against the corpus stats. If mean IDF > threshold (or any token IDF > X), set keyword_weight from default (e.g. 0.5) to 0.75 and enable Meilisearch phrase matching with quotes around top-2 rarest bigrams. Also enable matchingStrategy='all' for these queries.

action_plan.title: Penalize Apocrypha/secondary-canon when query matches primary canon
action_plan.description: Two Christian queries (Golden Rule, Beatitudes) and several Islamic/Hindu queries land in deuterocanonical or commentary works rather than the primary scripture. Add a soft penalty when a query semantically matches a known primary-canon passage but the top hit is from a secondary/commentary source.
action_plan.implementation: Tag documents with canonical_tier (gospel/quran/gita = 0, apocrypha/tafsir/commentary = 1, history = 2). In paragraph reranker, apply -0.15 score adjustment per tier-step when query content suggests primary scripture (detected via tradition-specific keyword lists: 'Blessed are', 'do unto', 'Krishna said', 'salat', 'zakat').
action_plan.success_metric: christian-golden-rule, christian-beatitudes-meek, hindu-bhagavad-gita-action, islamic-zakat-charity all pass

action_plan.title: Add eval-mode bypass and per-stage timing metrics
action_plan.description: Latency is now acceptable but p95 9.7s still has headroom. Carrying the prior recommendation forward: expose per-stage timings (intent, research, craft, rerank) in response headers and add ?mode=raw to skip jafar-pipeline entirely. This makes future tuning measurable and unblocks per-stage budgets.
action_plan.implementation: In api/lib/jafar-pipeline.js, wrap each stage with performance.now() and emit X-Stage-Timings header. Add mode=raw query param that calls hybridSearch directly and returns without intent/craft. Wire test runner to record these timings per fixture.
action_plan.success_metric: p95 latency visibility; potential 30-40% latency reduction in eval runs

notes_on_previous: The previous plan was on target: P1 (bypass pipeline / timeouts) eliminated the timeout class entirely (0 timeouts now vs 6 before), and overall pass rate moved 56%→71%. P3 (paragraph-level reranking) was correctly identified as the next big lever and is now the #1 priority — the failure pattern has crystallized around 'right book, wrong paragraph,' confirming that diagnosis. P4 (entity index) also remains valid and is reprioritized to #2 since entity-aware is the lowest-scoring category (56%). No dead ends; the restraint shown in run 1 (not tuning against noise) continues to pay dividends. P5 from the prior run (stage-level metrics) is still unimplemented and carried forward — cheap and worth doing before further tuning.
